objective: Introduce a repair-only role that keeps runs moving after repairable artifact validation failures without allowing code edits or review decisions.
goals: Route repairable invalid_artifact failures into a same-phase repair lane.
Limit the repairer to staged required artifacts for the current phase only.
Forbid the repairer from modifying product code, tests, configs, canonical artifacts, or run-state files.
Forbid the repairer from making review decisions such as verdict, must-fix, approval, or security status changes.
Commit repaired artifacts and continue phase progression without stopping the run when revalidation succeeds.

hard_constraints.title: Repairer must never edit product code
rules: Only current staged required artifacts may be edited.
src/, app/, prisma/, tests/, package.json, canonical artifacts, archive artifacts, run-state, and event logs are off limits.

hard_constraints.title: Repairer must never act as a reviewer
rules: Do not generate or reinterpret must_fix items.
Do not replace approval, reject, or rollback decisions.

hard_constraints.title: Repairer must avoid semantic rewrites
rules: Limit fixes to syntax, materialization, and schema-shape issues.
Immutable fields in reviewer artifacts must not change.
Meaningful content changes require a normal phase rerun or terminal failure.

findings.title: Validation failure currently becomes run failure immediately
impact: Provider jobs can succeed while the run still stops.
Self-running workflows are interrupted by small artifact defects.

findings.title: Syntax errors and semantic errors are treated the same
impact: Cheap syntax fixes trigger expensive full reruns or manual resume.
UX is worse than necessary for parse-only failures.

findings.title: Same-phase reruns are token-expensive
impact: The full phase prompt and evidence are resent even for local escape issues.
Latency and provider cost increase unnecessarily.

repairable: Materialization failures with recoverable raw staged text
terminal: Meaning-level contradictions between markdown and JSON
Evidence gaps that would require new judgment

flow: Errors are classified as repairable or terminal.
Repairable failures enter a same-phase repair lane instead of failing the run.
If valid, the normal commit and next-phase transition continue.
If repair budget is exhausted or the error is terminal, the run fails.

run_state.status: Keep running during repair when possible.

forbidden_actions: adding new evidence from unrelated files
writing canonical or archived artifacts directly

proposed_components.name: ArtifactRepairPolicy
proposed_components.responsibility: Classify failures, define immutable fields, and manage repair budget.

proposed_components.name: ArtifactRepairTransaction
proposed_components.responsibility: Run the repair lane and revalidation for the same phase.

proposed_components.name: RepairPromptBuilder
proposed_components.responsibility: Build a repair-only prompt from validation context and staged artifacts.

proposed_components.name: RepairDiffGuard
proposed_components.responsibility: Block immutable field changes and semantic drift.

workstreams.title: Integrate repair transaction into phase execution
Add deterministic pre-fix before invoking repairer
Add repair budget and circuit breaker

tests: Repairable bad-escape JSON error is fixed without failing the run.
Repairer cannot mutate immutable reviewer fields such as verdict or security status.
Terminal semantic invariant violations bypass the repair lane and fail normally.
Reviewer artifact syntax-only repair preserves verdict and status fields.
Known implementer schema-shape mismatches can be repaired without semantic changes.
Repeated identical repair failures hit the repair budget and stop.

rollout.scope: Deterministic pre-fix only, no repairer yet.
Artifact-only repairer for JSON and schema repairs with immutable field guards.

success_metrics: Repairable invalid_artifact failures no longer require manual resume in common cases.
Same-phase full reruns become less frequent for syntax-only failures.
No reviewer semantic drift incidents caused by repair.
conclusion: A third role is worthwhile only as a narrow artifact-repair lane. The engine must enforce that the repairer never edits product code and never performs review decisions.
